citation: 11 U.S.C. (Bankruptcy Code, formerly Bankruptcy Act); cf. 18-B M.R.S. § 816 (Maine Uniform Trust Code trustee powers)
current_form: 11 U.S.C. (federal Bankruptcy Code); cf. 18-B M.R.S. § 816 (Specific powers of trustee)
note: Federal Bankruptcy Code is current; the modern title is the Bankruptcy Code (11 U.S.C.), the 1898 Bankruptcy Act was repealed by the 1978 Code. 18-B (Maine Uniform Trust Code) was NOT recodified into 18-C and remains current; 18-B § 816 'Specific powers of trustee' is in force.
Maine Business Corporation Act, ch. 7 (Shareholders). Section 701 'Annual meeting' is current. Title 13-C replaced the older Title 13-A BCA effective 2003.

current_form: 13-C M.R.S. § 701 (Annual meeting of shareholders); § 801 (Requirement for and duties of board of directors)
note: Both sections current. Note label nuance: § 801 is 'Requirement for and duties of board of directors' (board management), not a 'director meeting' provision; director-meeting mechanics live in §§ 820-824 (e.g., § 821 Meetings). Section number is valid; descriptor is loose.

current_form: 13-C M.R.S. § 704 (Action without meeting / written consent of shareholders)
note: Current. Governs shareholder action by unanimous written consent in lieu of a meeting.
Both current within ch. 7 (Shareholders).

current_form: 14 M.R.S. § 6002 (Tenancy at will; buildings on land of another)
note: Current and frequently amended. Governs notice to quit for tenancy at will (general 30-day written notice; 7-day for nonpayment), and requires notice language advising the tenant of the right to contest in court before an FED action under ch. 709. Confirm exact day-count/wording against the live text for any given clause, as the notice requirements have been amended repeatedly through the 131st/132nd Legislatures.

The Maine Bail Code remains codified at Title 15, ch. 105-A; current section span is 1001-1105 (range still includes 1001-1104). NOT renumbered out of ch. 105-A, but SUBSTANTIVELY AMENDED multiple times since 2018 (notably the 2023-2024 bail reforms, e.g., PL 2023 changes to pretrial release/conditions). Treat any pinpoint subsection cite as needing re-check against current text.
Chapter heading and section range valid; ch. 105-A now runs to § 1105. Substantively amended since 2018 (bail reform); verify pinpoint citations.

The CHAPTER survives but the '(Supp. 1999)' pinpoint is badly stale. The Bail Code has been amended many times since 1999, including major 2023-2024 reforms; a 1999 supplement cite no longer reflects current law. Replace the parenthetical date.

citation: 18-A M.R.S. Art. 5, Pt. 9 (Maine Uniform Power of Attorney Act); cf. 18-C M.R.S. § 5-905 (current notice-to-principal requirement)

note: POA Act moved from 18-A to 18-C on 2019-07-01 (PL 2017 c.402). CRITICAL: the CURRENT statutory Notice to Principal and Notice to Agent in 18-C § 5-905 were UPDATED to read 'Maine Revised Statutes, Title 18-C, Article 5, Part 9' — they no longer say 18-A. A clause body printing 'Title 18-A, Article 5, Part 9' is STALE, not faithful to the current form.

18-A § 2-504 was 'Self-proved will'. In the 18-C recodification the self-proving-will provision is RENUMBERED to 18-C § 2-503. NOTE: 18-C § 2-504 is a DIFFERENT section ('Who may witness a will'). If this cite was meant for the self-proving will, the correct current cite is 18-C § 2-503, NOT 18-C § 2-504.
The 'now 18-C § 2-504' mapping is INCORRECT. 18-A § 2-504 (Self-proved will) became 18-C § 2-503; 18-C § 2-504 is instead 'Who may witness a will'. Use 18-C § 2-503 for the self-proving will.
The stated successor 18-C § 2-504 is WRONG for the self-proving will. Self-proved will = 18-C § 2-503; 18-C § 2-504 = 'Who may witness a will'.

18-A § 5-804 was 'Optional form' (the statutory advance health-care directive form). In 18-C the Health-Care Decisions Act sections shifted: the optional FORM is now 18-C § 5-805 (NOT 18-C § 5-804, which is 'Revocation of advance health care directive').

note: Correctly identifies 18-A § 5-804 as the statutory form. Current cite for the optional advance-directive form is 18-C § 5-805, not 18-C § 5-804 (revocation).

current_form: 24 C.F.R. Part 35 (HUD Lead-Based Paint Poisoning Prevention in Certain Residential Structures); Subpart A implements the disclosure requirements of 42 U.S.C. 4852d on sale/lease of target housing.
note: Current in eCFR (Title 24 current as of 2026). No renumbering. Subpart A requires disclosure of known LBP/hazards, records, EPA pamphlet, 10-day inspection opportunity, and contract attachment/warning language; 3-year record retention.

current_form: 31 C.F.R. 1010.380, as amended by FinCEN's interim final rule published 90 FR 13688 (Mar. 26, 2025). The definition of 'reporting company' is revised to mean ONLY entities formed under foreign law and registered to do business in a U.S. State/Tribal jurisdiction ('foreign reporting companies'). Entities created in the U.S. ('domestic reporting companies') and U.S. persons are EXEMPT from BOI reporting.
note: KEY CHANGED-LAW FINDING. FinCEN interim final rule (effective Mar. 21, 2025; published Mar. 26, 2025) gutted the original BOI scope: domestic reporting companies and their beneficial owners no longer file; only foreign reporting companies report, and they need not report any U.S.-person beneficial owners. Deadlines for foreign companies: those registered before Mar. 26, 2025 had to file by Apr. 25, 2025; those registering on/after Mar. 26, 2025 file within 30 days of effective registration. As of 2026-06-01 the IFR remains OPERATIVE; FinCEN stated it intended to finalize but no superseding FINAL reporting rule has been located. Any pre-2025 advisory clause (e.g., the affected blocks) telling a typical Maine domestic LLC/corp it must file BOI is OUT OF DATE.

current_form: 31 M.R.S. § 1559 (Duties of members and other persons)
note: Current, in ch. 21 (the Maine Limited Liability Company Act, eff. 2011). Caption is 'Duties of members and other persons' (fiduciary duty/standard of care and protection from monetary liability). See descriptor note below.

citation: 31 M.R.S. § 1559 (Maine LLC Act — limitation of liability of members and managers)
current_form: 31 M.R.S. § 1559 (Duties of members and other persons)
note: Section current. Descriptor is imprecise: § 1559 addresses duties/standard of care and bars personal liability for breach of duty absent bad faith. The general member/manager NON-liability for LLC debts/obligations is § 1549. If the clause is about shielding members from LLC liabilities, § 1549 is the better cite; if about duty-of-care liability protection, § 1559 is correct.

current_form: 31 U.S.C. 5336 (Corporate Transparency Act) statute is unamended and remains valid, BUT its implementing regulation (31 C.F.R. 1010.380) was narrowed by FinCEN's Mar. 2025 interim final rule so that in practice only foreign reporting companies must file BOI; domestic companies are exempt.
note: Statute itself is intact; the practical reporting obligation changed at the regulatory level (see 31 C.F.R. 1010.380). Domestic reporting companies and U.S. persons exempted as of the Mar. 2025 IFR; still operative in 2026. Advisory clauses must be updated.

current_form: 31 U.S.C. 5336 (CTA) unchanged as a statute; implementing rule 31 C.F.R. 1010.380 narrowed by FinCEN Mar. 2025 IFR to cover only foreign reporting companies, exempting U.S. domestic companies and U.S. persons.
note: See 31 C.F.R. 1010.380 entry. Mar. 2025 interim final rule exempts domestic reporting companies and U.S. persons; foreign reporting companies still report but not their U.S.-person owners. IFR remains operative in 2026; no superseding final reporting rule located. Pre-2025 BOI advisory blocks need revision.

current_form: CTA statute (31 U.S.C. 5336) intact; reg 31 C.F.R. 1010.380 amended by FinCEN interim final rule (Mar. 26, 2025) to limit 'reporting company' to foreign entities only; domestic companies and U.S. persons exempt from BOI reporting.
note: See 31 C.F.R. 1010.380 entry. Mar. 2025 IFR exempts U.S. domestic reporting companies and U.S. persons; only foreign reporting companies file (and not their U.S.-person beneficial owners). Operative in 2026; FinCEN intended to finalize but no superseding final reporting rule located. Update pre-2025 advisory clauses.

current_form: CTA statute (31 U.S.C. 5336) intact; reg 31 C.F.R. 1010.380 amended by FinCEN interim final rule (Mar. 26, 2025) to limit 'reporting company' to foreign entities only; domestic companies and U.S. persons exempt from BOI reporting.
note: See 31 C.F.R. 1010.380 entry. Mar. 2025 IFR exempts U.S. domestic reporting companies and U.S. persons; only foreign reporting companies file. IFR remains operative in 2026; no superseding final reporting rule located. Pre-2025 BOI advisory blocks (the affected blocks) are out of date.

current_form: CTA statute (31 U.S.C. 5336) intact; reg 31 C.F.R. 1010.380 amended by FinCEN interim final rule (Mar. 26, 2025) to limit 'reporting company' to foreign entities only; U.S. domestic companies and U.S. persons exempt from BOI reporting.
note: See 31 C.F.R. 1010.380 entry. Mar. 2025 IFR exempts domestic reporting companies and U.S. persons; only foreign reporting companies report. Operative in 2026; no superseding final reporting rule located. Revise pre-2025 advisory clauses.

Current. Requires acknowledgment of deeds/instruments before recording; expressly accepts acknowledgments conforming to RULONA (Title 4, ch. 39).

Current. Maine Condominium Act spans §§ 1601-101 through 1604-118, governing condominiums created on/after Jan 1, 1983.

current_form: 36 M.R.S. §§ 4641 to 4641-N (Real Estate Transfer Tax, ch. 711-A)
note: Truncated input string ('4641-'). Range current. RETT in ch. 711-A runs §§ 4641 (Definitions) through 4641-N. Rate amended: effective Nov 1, 2025 an additional $3.80/$500 applies to value over $1M (PL 2025).

current_form: 36 M.R.S. §§ 4641 to 4641-N (Real Estate Transfer Tax, ch. 711-A)
note: Current. Base rate $2.20 per $500 (split grantor/grantee); high-value surtax of $3.80 per $500 over $1M for transfers on/after Nov 1, 2025. Substantively amended since 2018 (rate add-on).

current_form: 36 M.R.S. § 5250-A (Withholding on sales of real estate)
note: Truncated input string ('5250-'). Intended cite is § 5250-A (nonresident real estate withholding, 2.5% on consideration of $100,000+). Current.

current_form: 36 M.R.S. § 5250-A (Withholding on sales of real estate)
note: Current. Buyer must withhold 2.5% from nonresident seller when consideration >= $100,000; remit with Form REW-1 within 30 days of closing.

current_form: 4 M.R.S. ch. 39 (Revised Uniform Law on Notarial Acts), §§ 1901-1933
note: REPEALED/SUPERSEDED. Old § 1014 was in Title 4, ch. 22 (Uniform Recognition of Acknowledgments Act, §§ 1011-1019), which was repealed when RULONA (ch. 39) took effect July 1, 2023 (PL 2021, c. 651). Acknowledgment requirements now flow through RULONA (e.g., §§ 1902, 1905) and 33 M.R.S. § 203. Re-cite to RULONA.

note: Current in eCFR. No renumbering. Mirrors the HUD/EPA Title X joint disclosure rule with the EPA counterpart provisions.

current_form: 42 U.S.C. 4852d (Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act of 1992, Title X) - disclosure of known lead-based paint/hazards on sale or lease of target housing; implemented jointly by HUD (24 C.F.R. Part 35) and EPA (40 C.F.R. Part 745).
note: Statute current and unchanged; no renumbering. Underlies both the HUD and EPA disclosure rules.

current_form: Title X lead disclosure framework: 42 U.S.C. 4852d (statute) + 24 C.F.R. Part 35 Subpart A (HUD) + 40 C.F.R. Part 745 Subpart F (EPA), all current.
note: All three citations current in eCFR as of 2026; no renumbering. Disclosure, EPA pamphlet, 10-day inspection opportunity, contract attachment, and 3-year retention remain the operative requirements.

citation: 42 U.S.C. § 4852d; 24 C.F.R. Part 35; 40 C.F.R. Part 745 (Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act / Title X)

note: All current in eCFR as of 2026; no renumbering. Parenthetical naming (Title X) is accurate.

current_form: HIPAA: Privacy Rule = 45 C.F.R. Part 160 + Part 164 Subparts A & E; Security Rule = 45 C.F.R. Part 160 + Part 164 Subparts A & C; Breach Notification = Part 164 Subpart D; Enforcement = Part 160 Subparts C-E.
note: Current in eCFR (Title 45 last amended May 26, 2026). Parts 160 and 164 remain the correct citation; no renumbering. Note HHS proposed Security Rule amendments were pending in this period, but the cited Parts/numbering are unchanged.

current_form: Kyles v. Whitley, 514 U.S. 419 (1995) - Brady materiality assessed cumulatively; good law.

The Maine Rules of Criminal Procedure were replaced statewide by the Maine Rules of Unified Criminal Procedure effective July 1, 2015 (M.R.U.Crim.P. 1(e)). The current criminal-discovery rule is M.R.U.Crim.P. 16 ('Discovery by the Defendant'), with companion Rule 16A ('Discovery by the State'). Cite in the OLD 'M.R. Crim. P.' form is superseded; update to M.R.U.Crim.P. 16(a).
Superseded by M.R.U.Crim.P. 16 effective 7/1/2015 statewide. 16(a) = 'Automatic Discovery'; 16(b) = 'Time for Providing Automatic Discovery'. Update prefix to M.R.U.Crim.P.

citation: Me. Dist. Ct. Civ. R. 1.10; Me. R. Civ. P. 33; Me. Fam. Div. R. 1.25
current_form: Me. R. Civ. P. 33 is current; the 'Me. Dist. Ct. Civ. R. 1.10' and 'Me. Fam. Div. R. 1.25' cites do not correspond to any current Maine rule numbering
note: Maine has NO standalone 'District Court Civil Rules' or 'Family Division Rules' rule set. Family Division procedure lives in the Maine Rules of Civil Procedure, Chapter XIII (Rules 100-129; e.g., Rule 100 'Scope of the Family Division Rules', Rule 112 'Discovery'); there is no Maine rule numbered 1.10 or 1.25. A standalone 'Maine Rules of Family Procedure' using 1.x numbering was PROPOSED in 2025 (comments due June 9, 2025) but is NOT shown as adopted/effective on the courts.maine.gov rules index as of 2026-06-01 (only MRCivP 100-101 were amended eff. 6/1/2026). FLAG: the 1.10/1.25 cite forms are non-current; embedded 'Me. R. Civ. P. 33' is fine.

current_form: Rules of the Circuit Court of the State of New Hampshire -- Family Division (current set); but the Family Division is being abolished effective Jan 1, 2026

FLAG: NH HB652 (2025) abolishes the Family Division and reassigns its jurisdiction effective Jan 1, 2026, so this rule set and its numbering are in transition as of the verification date; confirm whether Rule 1.2 survived the reorganization before relying on it. Brief currency check only, as instructed.

current_form: Title 18-C (Maine Probate Code); trust provisions remain in Title 18-B
note: Title 18-A (the former Maine Probate Code) was repealed/recodified effective 2019-07-01 by PL 2017 c.402. Documents executed on/after that date should cite Title 18-C. Bare 'Title 18-A' in a current document body is STALE.
Title 18-B (Maine Uniform Trust Code) is CURRENT and was not affected by the 18-A→18-C recodification. Trust matters still cite 18-B.
Title 18-C is the CURRENT Maine Probate Code, effective 2019-07-01 (PL 2017 c.402). Correct title for post-2019 probate documents.
